title: Starbucks Chicago Roastery ABM Data
subtitle: Floor structure, menu, 70 Yelp reviews & transition matrices
description:
## What's inside

Data for building an Agent-Based Model of customer flow through the Starbucks Reserve Roastery Chicago — the world's largest Starbucks (35,000 sq ft, 5 floors).

### Files
1. **roastery_floors.csv** — 5-floor structure with functions, features, dwell times, peak hours
2. **roastery_menu.csv** — 46 menu items by floor with nutrition and estimated prices
3. **floor_adjacency_prior.csv** — Physical adjacency-based transition probability matrix
4. **reviews_manual.csv** — 70 manually collected Yelp reviews (Apr 2025 – Mar 2026)

## Data Sources

| File | Source | License |
|---|---|---|
| Floor structure | Starbucks Reserve official site, media coverage | Public |
| Menu items | Starbucks Reserve menus (starbucksreserve.com) | Public |
| Reviews | Yelp (manually collected, fair use for research) | Fair use |
| Adjacency matrix | Derived from building layout | Original |

## Related notebooks (7-notebook series)

| # | Notebook | Description |
|---|----------|-------------|
| 1 | Roastery Data | Floor structure, menu, adjacency matrix |
| 2 | Review NLP | Floor/product extraction, transition sequences |
| 3 | Transition Calibration | Bayesian blending of prior + observed |
| 4 | ABM Simulation | 5000-agent customer flow simulation |
| 5 | Association Analysis | Product co-mention network, cross-sell |
| 6 | Sensitivity Analysis | Perturbation, convergence, validation |
| 7 | Strategic Recommendations | Flow, revenue, experience optimization |
